reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding the kernel boot argument fips=1 and changing the system-wide cryptographic policy. This change is not supported on an already installed system, can cause boot failures, break applications that rely on non‑FIPS algorithms, and may lock out remote access. The policy explicitly classifies FIPS changes as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; potential boot failure; incompatible software libraries; loss of SSH/console access; system-wide cryptographic policy change
